V-M-059.checks: Admin-configured @MTProxyBot promotion tag is validated, redacted, audited, and propagated to runtime safely without changing issued proxy links.
V-M-059.commands: pass-live 2026-05-23: node scripts/phase42-mtproto-promotion-tag-smoke.mjs && cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_promotion_tag.py -v; operator clean-deploy validation confirmed @MTProxybot registration, MTPROTO_AD_TAG RU/DE restart application, and applied state.
V-M-059.evidence: Promotion tag validation/storage/pending-restart tests, static smoke, runtime compile, and operator clean-deploy validation passed; full tags remain masked in API/audit-facing state, and the documented fake-TLS inner 32-hex secret registration flow is confirmed.

verification-levels.module-local: Validate tag parser, masking, storage state, audit payloads, runtime apply state, env fallback, and redaction fixtures.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run with V-M-057 and V-M-058 to prove admin API/UI use the same masked state and confirmation contract.
verification-levels.phase-level: Live promotion tag rollout requires operator-provided @MTProxyBot tag, runtime apply or planned restart state, and post-change req_pq proof.

module-checks.check-2: Zero 32-hex fallback remains valid when no promotion channel is configured.
module-checks.check-3: Blank, malformed, non-hex, wrong-length, and whitespace-padded invalid tags are rejected before runtime config.
module-checks.check-4: Stored and returned admin state contains masked tag only.
module-checks.check-5: Audit records who changed the tag and when, without storing the full tag.
module-checks.check-6: Runtime apply reports applied, pending_restart, or failed without rotating base secret, salt, SNI, assignment ownership, or issued links.
module-checks.check-7: Deploy/runtime env paths keep a non-blank PROXY_AD_TAG/MTPROTO_AD_TAG value.
module-checks.check-8: Redaction smoke fails if the full promotion tag appears in logs, API responses, admin UI fixtures, or runtime status.

scenarios.scenario-1: Valid 32-hex tag from @MTProxyBot is accepted and stored masked in admin state.
scenarios.scenario-2: Blank or malformed tags are rejected before reaching runtime config.
scenarios.scenario-3: Zero 32-hex fallback remains valid when no promotion channel is configured.
scenarios.scenario-4: Runtime apply reports applied or pending_restart without rotating base secret, salt, SNI, or owner links.
scenarios.scenario-5: Audit and logs never contain the full promotion tag.
scenarios.scenario-6: Operator extracts the inner 32-hex secret from one working ee fake-TLS proxy, registers it with @MTProxybot, receives a 32-hex promotion tag, restarts RU backend and DE runtime with MTPROTO_AD_TAG, and observes applied state.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Only masked promotion tag is exposed through API, UI, audit, logs, and runtime status.
redaction-rules.rule-2: The full tag may exist only in encrypted/config storage or runtime env material needed to operate the proxy.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Validation errors must not echo the submitted full tag.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Validator, backend API, smoke, and redaction tests may run locally with fake tags.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Applying a real @MTProxyBot tag or restarting runtime on production requires operator approval and post-change Telegram availability proof.
